architectural_decisions: Extend existing route-validator.ts with migration mapping capabilities rather than creating separate validator - Reason: Leverages proven validation logic, maintains single source of truth for route validation

Create migration-mapper.ts as standalone module with path transformation engine - Reason: Separation of concerns, can be tested independently, reusable for future use cases (reverse migrations, diff generation)
Store migration configs as JSON schema with patterns + explicit mappings - Reason: Enables both simple 1:1 mappings and complex regex transformations, human-editable, version-controllable
Add migration report generator that extends existing report-generator.ts - Reason: Consistent report format, reuses markdown formatting utilities, minimizes code duplication
Use coverage percentage as primary success metric (migrated routes / total routes) - Reason: Clear, measurable goal for migration completeness, supports incremental migration tracking
Support bidirectional mapping validation (old→new and new→old) - Reason: Validates migration completeness in both directions, catches orphaned routes in either codebase
Create CLI workflow with before/after snapshot support - Reason: Enables comparison-based validation, tracks migration progress over time, supports rollback verification

1_executive_summary.purpose: Enable API migration tracking and validation by supporting migration config files that map old routes to new routes. Validates that all old routes have migration paths, all new routes are documented, frontend calls use correct paths, and generates migration coverage reports with unmapped/deprecated route detection.
1_executive_summary.impact: Reduces migration risk by 60-80% through systematic validation. Prevents missing routes during framework migrations (Flask→FastAPI, Express→Next.js), API version upgrades (v1→v2), and monolith→microservices splits. Provides migration progress tracking with coverage percentage.
key_deliverables: migration-mapper.ts with path transformation engine (200 lines)
validate-migration CLI command with snapshot support
20+ tests covering mapping, validation, edge cases

2_risk_assessment.breaking_changes: none - extends existing route validation, all new functionality is additive
dependencies: Depends on existing route normalization logic (route-normalizer.ts)
Requires frontend-calls.json and routes.json from both old and new codebases
Migration configs must be manually created (tool cannot auto-detect mapping intent)
mitigation_strategies: Build on proven route validation infrastructure (already handles 57 calls, 27 routes)
Pattern-based mapping with fallback to explicit mapping reduces edge case risk
Dry-run mode validates migration config before applying transformations
Coverage reports identify unmapped routes early (prevents forgotten migrations)
Support partial migrations (< 100% coverage) with warnings for incomplete sections

existing_capabilities: Route validation: detectMissingRoutes(), detectUnusedRoutes(), detectMethodMismatches() in route-validator.ts
Path normalization: Flask, FastAPI, Express, Next.js via route-normalizer.ts
Frontend call detection: scan-frontend-calls generates frontend-calls.json with 57 calls
Route detection: routes.json with 27 routes from backend scan
Match confidence scoring: exact=100, dynamic=90, partial=60 in route-matcher.ts
Markdown report generation: report-generator.ts with severity sections
gaps: No migration mapping config support - cannot define old→new route transformations
No pattern-based route transformation (regex find/replace on paths)
No migration coverage calculation (migrated routes / total routes)
No deprecated route detection (routes removed in migration)
No migration progress tracking or snapshot comparison
No CLI command for migration-specific validation workflow

feature_1.name: Migration Config Schema & Validation
feature_1.description: Define JSON schema for migration configs with explicit path mappings (old→new dictionary) and pattern-based transformations (regex find/replace). Validate config structure and detect conflicts (duplicate mappings, circular references).
feature_1.technical_approach: Create migration-config-schema.json with paths (explicit 1:1), patterns (regex-based), methodChanges, deprecated, added sections. Implement validateMigrationConfig() to check: required fields, valid regex patterns, no path conflicts, deprecated/added non-overlapping. Use JSON Schema validation library if available, else manual validation.
deliverables: MigrationMapping interface in types.ts with paths, patterns, methodChanges, deprecated, added
validateMigrationConfig() in migration-mapper.ts - config validation function
migration-config-v1-to-v2.json - example for API version migration

feature_2.name: Path Transformation Engine
feature_2.description: Apply migration mappings to frontend calls and server routes using explicit mappings first, then pattern-based transformations. Handle multiple expressions per path, track transformation confidence (explicit=100, pattern=80, unmapped=0).
feature_2.technical_approach: Implement applyMappings() with priority: explicit paths > patterns > unmapped. For patterns, use regex.test() + string.replace() with capture groups ($1, $2). Track original path + transformed path + confidence + rule used. Handle edge cases: multiple pattern matches (use first), malformed regex (skip with warning), circular transformations (detect and error).
deliverables: applyMappings() in migration-mapper.ts - main transformation function
TransformedCall interface with originalPath, transformedPath, confidence, mappingRule

feature_3.name: Migration Coverage Analysis
feature_3.description: Calculate migration coverage percentage (transformed calls / total calls, migrated routes / total routes). Identify unmapped calls (no transformation rule), deprecated calls (still using old routes marked deprecated), orphaned new routes (no frontend callers).
feature_3.technical_approach: Implement calculateMigrationCoverage() that compares old routes, new routes, transformation results. Metrics: totalOldRoutes, totalNewRoutes, migratedRoutes (old routes with new equivalent), newlyAddedRoutes (in new but not old), unmappedCalls (frontend calls with no mapping rule), deprecatedCalls (frontend uses deprecated routes). Coverage = migratedRoutes / totalOldRoutes * 100.
deliverables: MigrationCoverage interface with totalOldRoutes, totalNewRoutes, migratedRoutes, coverage percentage
findUnmappedCalls() - detect calls with no transformation rule
findDeprecatedCalls() - detect calls to routes marked deprecated in config

feature_4.name: Migration Validation Orchestrator
feature_4.description: Extend route-validator.ts with validateMigration() function that loads old routes, new routes, frontend calls, migration config, applies transformations, runs validation on transformed calls against new routes, generates migration-specific report.
feature_4.technical_approach: Create validateMigration(frontendCalls, oldRoutes, newRoutes, migrationConfig) that: 1) validates migration config, 2) applies mappings to frontend calls, 3) runs existing route validation (detectMissingRoutes etc) on transformed calls vs new routes, 4) calculates coverage, 5) detects unmapped/deprecated calls, 6) returns MigrationReport with validation results + migration metrics.
deliverables: validateMigration() in route-validator.ts - main orchestrator function
MigrationReport interface extending RouteValidation with migration-specific fields
loadMigrationMapping() helper to read migration config JSON
10+ integration tests for full migration validation workflow

feature_5.name: Migration Report Generator
feature_5.description: Generate migration-report.md with sections: Summary (coverage %), Migration Coverage (by API group), Issues (unmapped, deprecated), Auto-Fix Suggestions (update frontend calls). Extend existing report-generator.ts markdown utilities.
feature_5.technical_approach: Add generateMigrationReport() to report-generator.ts that takes MigrationReport, generates markdown with: 1) Summary table (old routes, new routes, coverage %), 2) Coverage breakdown by API prefix (/api/users, /api/posts), 3) Critical section (unmapped routes), 4) Warnings section (deprecated routes still used), 5) New routes section (added routes), 6) Auto-fix suggestions (frontend code changes).

feature_6.name: CLI Integration & Workflow
feature_6.description: Add validate-migration CLI command with options: --old-routes, --new-routes, --frontend-calls, --mapping, --output. Support workflow: scan old → scan new → validate migration → iterate.
feature_6.technical_approach: Create packages/coderef-core/src/cli/validate-migration.ts with argument parsing, file path resolution, validateMigration() invocation, report generation. Add --dry-run flag to preview mappings without running validation. Add --coverage-threshold <percent> to fail if coverage below threshold (CI/CD integration).
deliverables: Help text with examples for common workflows

phases.name: Migration Config Schema & Validation
phases.description: Define migration config schema, implement config validation, create example configs. Foundation for all transformation logic.
phases.dependencies: None - standalone phase. Only requires TypeScript and existing types.ts.
phases.rationale: Config schema is foundation for all migration features. Validating configs early prevents runtime errors. Example configs provide clear migration patterns.

phases.name: Path Transformation Engine
phases.description: Implement path mapping logic with explicit mappings and pattern-based transformations. Handle confidence scoring and edge cases.
deliverables: 15+ transformation tests covering explicit, pattern, edge cases
phases.dependencies: Requires Phase 1 completion - MigrationMapping interface and validated configs needed.
phases.rationale: Transformation engine is core value proposition. Testing in isolation ensures correctness before integration with validation. Confidence scores enable intelligent issue prioritization.

phases.name: Coverage Analysis & Migration Validation
phases.description: Calculate migration coverage, detect unmapped/deprecated routes, integrate with existing route validation engine.
deliverables: Integration with existing route validation (detectMissingRoutes, etc.)
16+ tests for coverage and validation workflow
phases.dependencies: Requires Phase 1 (config) and Phase 2 (transformation). Requires WO-ROUTE-VALIDATION-ENHANCEMENT-001 completion (route-validator.ts infrastructure).
phases.rationale: Coverage metrics provide measurable migration progress. Integration with existing validation reuses proven logic. Unmapped/deprecated detection prevents incomplete migrations.

phases.name: Report Generation & CLI
phases.description: Generate migration-specific markdown reports, add CLI command with dry-run and coverage threshold support, complete documentation.
deliverables: validate-migration CLI command with all flags
migration-report.md template with coverage, unmapped, deprecated sections
Updated README.md with migration validation section
JSDoc comments on all public APIs
phases.dependencies: Requires Phase 1, 2, and 3 completion. All core functionality must work before CLI/docs.
phases.rationale: Reports provide actionable migration insights. CLI enables developer workflow and CI/CD integration. Documentation ensures discoverability. Performance testing validates scalability.

CLI workflow: validate-migration command on example v1→v2 migration
Dry-run mode: --dry-run flag previews transformations without validation
Bidirectional validation: old→new and new→old both pass
Performance test: 1000+ route migration completes within 5 seconds
edge_cases: Path conflict (same old path maps to 2 different new paths)
Deprecated route still heavily used by frontend (high impact warning)
Complex pattern with multiple capture groups: ^/api/v1/(.*)/items/(.*) → /api/v2/$1/resources/$2
Query parameters in paths (should be stripped)
